blocks.content: The Catalyst Optimizer is what separates Spark's DataFrame API from an ordinary distributed loop. It transforms your high-level query through 4 phases before a single byte of data is processed.

rows: Column names validated against schema. Unknown functions looked up. Types resolved.
Rule-based rewrites: predicate pushdown, column pruning, constant folding, Boolean simplification.
Cost-based: picks join strategies (broadcast vs SMJ), aggregate strategies. Generates multiple candidates, selects lowest cost.
Tungsten whole-stage codegen: compiles tight loops to JVM bytecode. Eliminates virtual function call overhead.

blocks.content: The logical optimization phase applies a library of rules repeatedly until no more apply. The most impactful ones:

rows: Moves filter() down past joins/scans closer to source
If you select 3 cols from a 50-col table, Catalyst drops the other 47 from the scan
Evaluates constant expresions at plan time
Reorders multi-way joins to minimize intermediate sizes (CBO)
Smaller table joined first; join order based on statistics

blocks.content: Rule-based vs Cost-based optimization: Most rules are rule-based (always applies). Cost-based optimization (CBO) uses column statistics (cardinality, distinct values, histograms) collected via ANALYZE TABLE ... COMPUTE STATISTICS. Without statistics, join reorder falls back to plan order. At MAANG, always run ANALYZE on large Delta tables before complex multi-way joins.

Whole-Stage Codegen (Tungsten Phase 2) compiles an entire pipeline of operators into a single JVM method with a tight loop — eliminating virtual function calls between operators, reducing object allocation, and enabling CPU branch prediction and cache locality.

# Codegen in explain output:
df.explain()
# You'll see operators prefixed with *(N):
# *(1) Project [name#5, salary#6]        <- codegen stage 1
# +- *(1) Filter (salary#6 > 80000)      <- same codegen stage
#    +- *(1) FileScan parquet [name, salary, dept]
#
# Operators in the same *(N) stage run as one compiled JVM method.
# Exchange (shuffle) breaks the codegen stage — it's *(1) then *(2).

# Disable codegen to debug (don't do this in production):
spark.conf.set('spark.sql.codegen.wholeStage', 'false')

In explain() output, operators prefixed with *(1), *(2) etc. are in a whole-stage codegen pipeline. Exchange (shuffle) breaks stages. An InputAdapter wraps operators that can't be code-generated (certain Python UDFs, complex types).

rows: Forces broadcast join for a specific table
ANALYZE TABLE t COMPUTE STATISTICS FOR COLUMNS col1, col2
Debug — see static plan without runtime adaptation
Avoid re-shuffle if you re-use the same join key multiple times
Cross joins create massive intermediate — Catalyst warns but doesn't prevent

rune_cards.definition: Spark's query optimizer framework. Built in Scala with Scala macros. 4 phases: analysis → logical opt → physical planning → codegen. Extensible — Databricks adds Delta-specific rules.
Moves WHERE clauses as close to the data source as possible. For Parquet, pushes all the way to the file reader — row groups with all values outside the predicate range are skipped without reading.
Optimizer uses actual table statistics (row counts, column cardinality, histograms) to choose join order and strategy. Requires running ANALYZE TABLE ... COMPUTE STATISTICS first.
Compiles multiple DataFrame operators into one JVM method for each pipeline stage. Eliminates virtual calls between operators. Shown as *(N) prefix in explain() output.
A wrapper in the physical plan for operators that can't participate in whole-stage codegen (some UDFs, python operations). Acts as a boundary — codegen stage ends here.

quiz.question: You run: df.filter(col('salary') > 80000).join(df2, 'dept').filter(col('active') == True). What does Catalyst's predicate pushdown do?
options: Runs all three operations in order as written
Pushes both filters before the join, reducing the data size that needs to be shuffled for the join
Moves the join before both filters to enable broadcast
Only pushes the second filter (active) — the first filter can't be moved
quiz.explanation: Catalyst pushes filters that reference only one table to BEFORE the join. salary > 80000 only touches the left table — it's pushed down to filter it before joining. active == True (if it references either table's column) is also pushed pre-join. This reduces the number of rows shuffled for the join, dramatically improving performance.

quiz.question: You see *(2) SortMergeJoin in your explain output. What does the *(2) prefix mean?
options: This is the 2nd join in the query
The SortMergeJoin operator is inside Whole-Stage Codegen stage 2 — compiled together with surrounding operators into one JVM method
This is the 2nd physical plan candidate considered
quiz.explanation: *(N) in explain output means the operator is part of Whole-Stage Codegen pipeline N — compiled into a single JVM tight loop with all adjacent operators sharing the same codegen stage. Exchange nodes (shuffles) separate codegen stages.
